checks.question: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?

findings.detail: Checked the revised migration path against the current callers in `LoraSelectorModal.tsx`, `useLightboxNavigation.ts`, and `useLightboxShellInteractionHandlers.ts`. The plan now adds an explicit bridge layer under `src/shared/components/ui/overlay/` (`OverlayInstanceContext`, `useCurrentOverlayIsTopmost()`, and `useOverlayElementIsTopmost(ref)`), which is a concrete way to migrate the existing `contentRef`-based dialog case and the callback-only lightbox hooks without prop-threading overlay ids through feature code.

Checked the plan's ownership story against the actual wrapper comments in `src/shared/components/ui/dialog.tsx` and the raw `LightboxShell.tsx` implementation. The approved requirement says the new stack should be the single automated owner of `document.body.style.pointerEvents` and focus restoration, but the plan never states how Base UI's own body/focus lifecycle will be disabled or contained even though the existing dialog wrapper comment explicitly notes that Base UI itself releases focus scope and body pointer-events during close. Without an explicit containment step, the milestone still only partially addresses the 'single owner' requirement.

checks.question: Are the proposed changes technically correct?

findings.detail: Checked the proposed registration lifecycle against the current dialog wrapper shape. Step 3 still says each wrapper 'calls `pushOverlay` on mount; `popOverlay` on unmount', but `src/shared/components/ui/dialog.tsx` intentionally keeps the `Dialog` root mounted while `open` toggles and many callers only conditionally render `DialogContent`. If overlay registration follows React mount/unmount instead of the actual popup/backdrop open lifecycle, the stack can keep stale entries during closed-but-mounted roots or animated teardown and produce incorrect topmost, pointer-events, or focus-restore behavior.

checks.question: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?

findings.detail: Checked the raw-owner inventory in the current repo with the same Base UI grep the brief cites. There are 9 matches today (`LightboxShell.tsx` plus the existing shared wrapper files like `dialog.tsx`, `popover.tsx`, `select.tsx`, `dropdown-menu.tsx`, `tooltipPrimitives.tsx`, `alert-dialog.tsx`, `hover-card.tsx`, and the type-only `command.tsx` import), so the repository is already mostly centralized at the wrapper layer. That makes the plan's repeated framing around '20+ callsites' and large one-primitive-at-a-time downstream callsite migration stale relative to the actual repo surface and risks unnecessary churn outside the remaining raw-owner and helper hotspots.

Checked the broader overlay-helper surface that earlier critiques called out. The revised plan now explicitly includes `src/shared/lib/interactions/elementPolicy.ts` and `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMobileInteractions.ts`, so the previously-missed popup-marker helper paths are no longer being treated as out of scope.

checks.question: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?

findings.detail: Checked the CSS-side marker contract, not just the JS helpers. Beyond the files the plan names (`src/styles/components/interactions.css` and `src/index.css`), `[data-dialog-content]` is also styled in `src/styles/components/dialog-overrides.css` and `src/styles/components/wes-surfaces.css`, and `[role="listbox"]` gets special treatment in `src/styles/foundations/base-case.css`. Because M2 explicitly preserves or migrates marker contracts, those stylesheet consumers are supporting infrastructure too; the plan's CSS inventory is still incomplete.

Checked the actual caller-side layering overrides with a repo-wide grep for `zIndexBase`, `overlayClassName`, and `z-[...]` on overlay callsites. The plan names a few first-wave examples, but the current repo still has many numeric z-index overrides on overlay consumers such as `ProfitSplitBar.tsx`, `VariantSelector.tsx`, several media-lightbox control files, `sharedSections.tsx`, and multiple tooltip-heavy components. Since the validation step proposes a wide grep over `src/shared/components`, `src/domains`, and `src/tools`, the migration checklist is not yet exhaustive enough to make that invariant realistically pass.

checks.question: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?

findings.detail: Checked the actual layering-related caller arguments rather than only the topmost-gating hooks. `LoraSelectorModal.tsx` is the only current `DialogContent` caller using the wrapper's bespoke `overlayClassName` and `zIndexBase` props, but many `TooltipContent` and `HoverCardContent` callers inject numeric z-order through ordinary `className` strings such as `className="z-[100001]"`, while `SelectContent` and `PopoverContent` callers commonly pass `container`, placement, and variant props. The plan correctly says container and placement props must survive, but it still under-specifies how the migration will identify and remove all layering-only `className` overrides across tooltip and hover-card callers without sweeping up unrelated styling classes.

Checked the original topmost-gating callers from the prior flags. The new bridge hooks in the revised plan are shaped to fit the real caller contracts: `LoraSelectorModal.tsx` can stay ref-based, and the lightbox hooks can read overlay-topmost state from context instead of receiving new arguments. That part of the caller story is now concretely addressed.

flags.concern: Overlay ownership: the plan still does not state how Base UI's own body pointer-events and focus-restoration behavior will be disabled or contained, so the proposed overlay stack may end up fighting the library instead of becoming the sole owner.
flags.evidence: `src/shared/components/ui/dialog.tsx` explicitly documents that Base UI releases focus scope and `body` pointer-events during close, but the revised plan only adds a store owner and does not name any wrapper/API step that neutralizes the library's built-in ownership.

flags.concern: Overlay migration scope: the plan still assumes a 20+ direct-callsite migration even though the current repo has only 9 raw Base UI overlay-import sites and is already mostly centralized through shared wrapper files.
flags.evidence: `rg 'from .*@base-ui/react/(dialog|popover|select|menu|tooltip|alert-dialog|context-menu|popup|preview-card)' src/ --type ts -l` returns 9 files in this checkout, most of them existing wrapper implementations under `src/shared/components/ui/`.

flags.concern: Overlay layering cleanup: the revised plan still does not enumerate the full caller surface for numeric z-index overrides, but its validation grep expects those overrides to be gone across broad directories.
flags.evidence: Current callers still pass z-order through `className` on overlay content in files such as `src/shared/components/ProfitSplitBar.tsx`, `src/shared/components/VariantSelector/VariantSelector.tsx`, `src/domains/media-lightbox/components/ButtonGroups.tsx`, and `src/domains/media-lightbox/components/editModes/sharedSections.tsx`, while step 10 proposes a wide `rg -n 'zIndexBase|overlayClassName|z-\[[0-9]+\]' ...` invariant.

flags.concern: Criterion 15: requires human verification (drive_browser, inspect_runtime_ui, verify_physical_device).
